channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: We’ll never be silenced, say Peter Akah, Mama Pee after EFCC grilling
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Activists Peter Akah and Mama Pee grilled by EFCC over naira abuse allegations, declare they will not be silenced and reaffirm support for Peter Obi.
Read More: https://punchng.com/well-never-be-silenced-say-peter-akah-mama-pee-after-efcc-grilling/

item.title: Yoruba lawyers call for NBA election postponement, demand NIN-based voter verification
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Yoruba Lawyers Forum calls for NBA election postponement, demanding NIN-based voter verification and electoral reforms to ensure a credible process.
Read More: https://punchng.com/yoruba-lawyers-call-for-nba-election-postponement-demand-nin-based-voter-verification/

item.title: Appeal Court voids Electoral Act provisions on party primaries, membership register
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Appeal Court in Abuja has struck down key Electoral Act provisions on party primaries and membership registers, citing inconsistency with the 1999 Cons
Read More: https://punchng.com/appeal-court-voids-electoral-act-provisions-on-party-primaries-membership-register/

item.title: French MPs Approve Assisted Dying Bill Amid Strict Conditions After Years of Debate
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: France’s National Assembly has approved a landmark bill to legalise assisted dying under strict conditions, marking a significant step in the country’s long-running debate over end-of-life care. Lawmakers voted 291 to 241 in favour of the legislation, which seeks to grant eligible adults with termin

item.title: Delta Youths Petitions National Assembly Over Alleged Fraud In NDDC internship Programme
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Anioma Youth Forum Worldwide has petitioned the Senate and the House of Representatives over alleged irregularities in the Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) Youth Internship Programme, calling for a comprehensive investigation into its implementation between August 2024 and July 2025. Th

item.title: Netflix is getting stingier about its viewing data, and Wall Street isn’t happy
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Netflix’s stock is falling in the wake of mixed earnings and a new plan to cut back on the publication of ‘What We Watched’ reports.

item.title: Lewandowski’s MLS debut postponed over air quality concerns
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Robert Lewandowski’s highly anticipated MLS debut was postponed due to severe air quality concerns from Canadian wildfires. Find out when he’ll play next.
Read More: https://punchng.com/lewandowskis-mls-debut-postponed-over-air-quality-concerns/

item.title: Trump tightens US visa rules, limits stay for foreign students and journalists
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Thursday finalized rules imposing stricter limits on how long foreign students and journalists may stay in the United States, tightening legal immigration. Student visas would be capped at four years, while journalists would be limited to 240-day stays. Chi

item.title: NBA to proceed with Saturday’s election, says SSS has released detained service provider’s MD
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: NBA said its NEC affirmed that there was no operational or logistical reason to postpone the election, now two days away. The post NBA to proceed with Saturday’s election, says SSS has released detained service provider’s MD appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Intuitive Surgical Drops as ACA Changes, GLP-1s Weigh on Growth
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Intuitive Surgical shares fell as much as 13% in after-market trading as growth in use of its da Vinci surgical robots slowed to the weakest pace in four years and its guidance for procedure growth was unchanged.

item.title: Bank Rally Hits Short Sellers Betting on Australia Lending Slump
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The upward march in share prices of Australia’s biggest banks is reversing the tumble sparked two months ago by property tax changes, and inflicting pain on short sellers who were anticipating a prolonged plunge.

item.title: At least one dead in Texas floods ravaging same area where campers died
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The same area devastated by floods last year's floods, which caused more than 130 deaths, is once again being hit.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Stocks in Asia look set to open lower Friday after a selloff in chipmakers hit Wall Street amid concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations.

item.description: The former CEO of Italy’s main highway operator has been given 12 years in prison.

item.title: US strikes in Iran hit airport, bridges, railway station
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Deadly US strikes overnight hit an airport, a railway station in the port city of Bandar Abbas and two bridges in southern Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian state media said Friday. The post US strikes in Iran hit airport, bridges, railway station appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil extended a weekly advance as the escalating conflict between the US and Iran raised concerns about disruptions to supply from the Middle East.

item.description: A security team comprising operatives of Department of State Security (DSS) and forest guards on Thursday rescued two victims of abduction in a forest between Oba Isin/Owa Onire area of isin/ Ifelodun Local Government Areas of Kwara State. The rescued children are 16-year-old Fatima Azeez and Ibrahi

item.description: The Nigeria Society of Physiotherapy (NSP) has said that the name of Mary Habila, the young woman who died at the residence of Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi in Ebonyi State, does not appear in the database of registered physiotherapists. This contradicts claims by the minister’s team

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Federal High Court in Kano has ruled that the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) lacks the legal authority to operate on state and local government roads, declaring the commission’s activities on township roads in Kano metropolis unlawful. item.title: UN finds looted gold, gum arabic bankrolling Sudan war
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Proceeds from looted gold and gum arabic are being used by warring parties in Sudan to fund the war, the UN human rights office has verified. item.title: Bwacha Names Aminu Zakari Running Mate For 2027 Taraba Governorship Election
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) governorship candidate in Taraba State for the 2027 general election, Senator Emmanuel Bwacha, has announced Engr. Aminu Zakari as his running mate The post Bwacha Names Aminu Zakari Running Mate For 2027 Taraba Governorship Election appeared first on Trending News

item.title: Donald Trump live: US president to deliver primetime address on elections
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Critics fear Trump may use his podium to spread spread doubt about election integrity before the 2026 midterms.

item.title: Tinubu Unveils $3.05bn Poverty Reduction Programmes
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has launched three intervention programmes valued at about $3.05bn to tackle poverty, strengthen healthcare and education, support displaced persons, and boost livelihoods across Nigeria. The programmes, unveiled on Thursday at the State House Conference Centre, Abuja, include

item.title: Dollar to Naira Exchange Rate Today, July 16, 2026: Nigerian Naira extends appreciation
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Naira continued to appreciate against the official foreign exchange market on Thursday for the second consecutive time this week. The Central Bank of Nigeria’s data showed that the Naira strengthened to N1,381.53 per dollar on Thursday, up from N1,382.18 traded on Wednesday. This means tha

item.title: X removes 4,000 creators from revenue-sharing programme for stealing content
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: X has stepped up efforts to clean up its creator revenue-sharing programme, removing nearly 4,000 accounts in a…

item.title: Fuel depots push petrol above N1,200/litre as Dangote halts gantry loading
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Private depot owners across Nigeria have begun quoting petrol at roughly N1,200 a litre, marketers said Thursday, after Dangote Petroleum read more Fuel depots push petrol above N1,200/litre as Dangote halts gantry loading

item.title: Why I won’t be at World Cup final – Argentina’s President
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Argentine President Javier Milei said Thursday he will not attend the World Cup 2026 final in the United States, preferring to continue watching his country take on Spain from home. The post Why I won’t be at World Cup final – Argentina’s President appeared first on Vanguard News.
